facts.statement: Turn 1 positioning - session framing phase requires hearing from both parties before domain work begins
Issue domain appears to involve post-separation financial arrangements and decision-making protocols
Spouse A states they need acknowledgment before they can engage with logistics.
Party B has not yet spoken — this is a structural constraint on all process-design options
Party A has set a sequencing condition: acknowledgment before logistics — this is not a constraint on option generation, but it is a constraint on how the mediator sequences the session
Spouse B has offered an acknowledgment of the harm caused.
facts.note: Accepted as a process-relevant acknowledgment. Not adjudicating the underlying relational claim.

facts.statement: Both parties identify the children's need for both parents to be able to function together.
Both parties have now participated substantively — the participation asymmetry that blocked option work at T3 is resolved
However, Party A has not yet confirmed that Party B's acknowledgment is sufficient — moving to options before this confirmation would violate Party A's stated threshold need
Spouse A agrees to begin discussing separation structure while reserving the right to return to the acknowledgment dimension.
Both parties identify minimising required joint decisions as the key structural preference.
Post-separation expense coordination with shared children — some joint decisions are legally unavoidable (major medical, education)
Minimizing joint decisions is achievable through autonomy zones, tiered thresholds, and default rules, but not through complete independence
Both parties accept the low-contact separation structure as a starting working basis.

facts_notes: Facts remain separated from unresolved feasibility questions.
